LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS FRANK FARRELL AND ISABELLE RYAN'S MOTION OBJECTING TO APEX SHEET METAL'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT AND SEEKING AN ORDER THAT THE SETTLEMENT PROCEEDS BE HELD IN TRUST FOR PLAINTIFFS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF PROOF THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS "OUT OF THE BALLPARK." (TECH-BILT, INV. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488, 499.) ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PROVIDED AUTHORITY FOR THE IMPOSITION OF THE REQUIREMENT THAT THE FUNDS BE HELD IN A TRUST. THE APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT OF CROSS-COMPLAINANT APEX SHEET METAL, INC. WITH JOSEPH J. HARTE DBA HARTE BUILDING & DESIGN IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFFs FRANK FARRELL and ISABELLE RYAN's Motion Objecting To Apex Sheet Metal's Notice Of Application For Good Faith Settlement And Seeking An Order That The Settlement Proceeds Be Held In Trust For Plaintiffs is Denied. Plaintiffs have not met their burden of proof that the settlement is "out of the ballpark." (Tech-Bilt, Inv. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Additionally, Plaintiffs have not provided authority for the imposition of the requirement that the funds be held in a trust. The Application for Determination of Good Faith Settlement of Cross-Complainant APEX Sheet Metal, INC. with Joseph J. Harte dba HARTE BUILDING & DESIGN is Granted. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H.
